mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of months where unemployment rises by more than 0.4 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand recovery, driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in >50% of observed quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflect supply-side bottlenecks that immediately pressure corporate earnings and cause negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth during tightening periods yields positive 5-day returns in >55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP growth rates signal expanding corporate revenue bases, which mechanically support higher equity valuations and generate positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth correlates with negative 20-day returns in >60% of quarterly observations.

mechanism_narrative: A widening spread between rising inflation and stagnant short-term rates erodes real yields, prompting portfolio rebalancing into equities and producing positive quarterly forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive CPIAUCSL growth combined with flat FEDFUNDS yields negative 60-day returns in >55% of months.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid payroll employment growth during a late-cycle expansion phase signals overheating economic activity that triggers central bank hawkish pivots, causing immediate negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Strong PAYEMS growth in late-cycle regimes produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of months.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below its 12-month moving average indicates a sustained supply-side contraction that reduces corporate profit margins and generates negative monthly equity returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO drops below its 12-month moving average
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in >55% of months where INDPRO trades below its 12-month trend.
